Five named steps borrowed from real\\n// watercolor terminology. Each maps to a multiplier that scales the\\n// underlying evaporation rate via rate = 0.9988^mult.\\n//\\n// \\\"Wet-on-wet\\\" is the default \u2014 it corresponds to mult=3, slightly\\n// faster than the original Curtis 0.9988 baseline (mult=1, \\\"Flooded\\\").\\n// The shift is intentional: the original baseline keeps the canvas\\n// wet for ~9.5 seconds, which feels indefinite in interactive use. At\\n// mult=3 (half-life ~3.2s) the canvas still bleeds beautifully but\\n// settles fast enough that performance stays comfortable during heavy\\n// brushwork. Users wanting the maximum-bleed Curtis behavior can pick\\n// \\\"Flooded\\\".\\nconst EVAP_PRESETS = {\\n  flooded:   1,    // half-life ~9.5s \u2014 pooled water, dramatic blooms\\n  wetOnWet:  3,    // half-life ~3.2s \u2014 soft Curtis-style bleed\\n  damp:      8,    // half-life ~1.2s \u2014 edges firmer, controlled\\n  dryBrush:  18,   // half-life ~0.5s \u2014 granular, paint sits in place\\n  boneDry:   50,   // half-life ~0.2s \u2014 almost dry-on-paper\\n};\\n\\nconst evapSelect = document.getElementById('evap-preset');\\nfunction setEvaporationMult(mult) {\\n  if (!isFinite(mult) || mult < 0.1) mult = 0.1;\\n  if (mult > 200) mult = 200;\\n  evaporationRate = Math.pow(0.9988, mult);\\n}\\nfunction applyEvapPreset(presetName) {\\n  const mult = EVAP_PRESETS[presetName];\\n  if (mult === undefined) return false;\\n  setEvaporationMult(mult);\\n  if (evapSelect && evapSelect.value !== presetName) evapSelect.value = presetName;\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\",\"stderr\":\"\"}",

+ padX * 2);\n  const h = Math.ceil(fontSize * 1.4) + padY * 2;\n  tmp.width = w;\n  tmp.height = h;\n  tctx.font = fontSpec;\n  tctx.textBaseline = 'middle';\n  tctx.fillStyle = '#000';\n brushPx = sampleStep * 1.6;\n\n  markCanvasActive();\n\n  const startX = centerGX - w * 0.5;\n  const startY = centerGY - h * 0.5;\n  let painted = 0;\n  for (let py = 0; py < h; py += sampleStep) {\n (gx < 0 || gx >= GW || gy < 0 || gy >= GH) continue;\n      paintAt(gx, gy, brushPx * pxToGridRadius, pigment, strength);\n      painted++;\n    }\n  }\n  return painted;\n}\n\n// ============================================================\n// SVG TRACING (v2 lib addition)\n// ============================================================\nconst SVG_TRACE_FIT = 0.70;\nconst SVG_POINTS_PER_FRAME = 10;\nconst SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS = 2.0;\nconst SVG_TRACE_STRENGTH = 0.42;\nconst SVG_TRACE_BRUSH_PX = 9;\n\nlet svgTraceState = null;\n\nfunction pathStringFromSvgElement(el) {\n  const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase();\n  if (tag === 'path') return el.getAttribute('d') || '';\n  if (tag === 'line') {\n    const x1 = el.getAttribute('x1') || 0, y1 = el.getAttribute('y1') || 0;\n    const x2 = el.getAttribute('x2') || 0, y2 = el.getAttribute('y2') || 0;\n    return 'M' + x1 + ' ' + y1 + ' L' + x2 + ' ' + y2;\n  }\n  if (tag === 'polyline' || tag === 'polygon') {\n    const pts = (el.getAttribute('points') || '').trim().split(/[\\s,]+/).map(Number);\n    if (pts.length < 4) return '';\n    let d = 'M' + pts[0] + ' ' + pts[1];\n    for (let i = 2; i + 1 < pts.length; i += 2) {\n      d += ' L' + pts[i] + ' ' + pts[i + 1];\n    }\n    if (tag === 'polygon') d += ' Z';\n    return d;\n  }\n  if (tag === 'rect') {\n    const x = +(el.getAttribute('x') || 0);\n    const y = +(el.getAttribute('y') || 0);\n    const w = +(el.getAttribute('width')  || 0);\n    const h = +(el.getAttribute('height') || 0);\n    return 'M' + x + ' ' + y + ' h' + w + ' v' + h + ' h' + (-w) + ' Z';\n  }\n  if (tag === 'circle') {\n    const cx = +(el.getAttribute('cx') || 0);\n    const cy = +(el.getAttribute('cy') || 0);\n    const r = +(el.getAttribute('r') || 0);\n    return 'M' + (cx - r) + ' ' + cy +\n      ' a' + r + ',' + r + ' 0 1,0 ' + (2 * r) + ',0' +\n      ' a' + r + ',' + r + ' 0 1,0 ' + (-2 * r) + ',0';\n  }\n  if (tag === 'ellipse') {\n    const cx = +(el.getAttribute('cx') || 0);\n    const cy = +(el.getAttribute('cy') || 0);\n    const rx = +(el.getAttribute('rx') || 0);\n    const ry = +(el.getAttribute('ry') || 0);\n    return 'M' + (cx - rx) + ' ' + cy +\n      ' a' + rx + ',' + ry + ' 0 1,0 ' + (2 * rx) + ',0' +\n      ' a' + rx + ',' + ry + ' 0 1,0 ' + (-2 * rx) + ',0';\n  }\n  return '';\n}\n\nfunction loadSVGAndTrace(svgText) {\n  const parser = new DOMParser();\n  const doc = parser.parseFromString(svgText, 'image/svg+xml');\n  const svg = doc.querySelector('svg');\n  if (!svg) throw new Error('No <svg> root found');\n\n  let viewBox = svg.getAttribute('viewBox');\n  let vbX = 0, vbY = 0, vbW = +(svg.getAttribute('width') || 100), vbH = +(svg.getAttribute('height') || 100);\n  if (viewBox) {\n    const parts = viewBox.trim().split(/[\\s,]+/).map(Number);\n    if (parts.length === 4) { vbX = parts[0]; vbY = parts[1]; vbW = parts[2]; vbH = parts[3]; }\n  }\n\n  const svgNS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';\n  const tmpSvg = document.createElementNS(svgNS, 'svg');\n  document.body.appendChild(tmpSvg);\n\n  const allPoints = [];\n  const geomEls = svg.querySelectorAll('path,line,polyline,polygon,rect,circle,ellipse');\n  for (const el of geomEls) {\n    const d = pathStringFromSvgElement(el);\n    if (!d) continue;\n    const p = document.createElementNS(svgNS, 'path');\n    p.setAttribute('d', d);\n    tmpSvg.appendChild(p);\n    let len = 0;\n    try { len = p.getTotalLength(); } catch (e) { tmpSvg.removeChild(p); continue; }\n    if (!isFinite(len) || len <= 0) { tmpSvg.removeChild(p); continue; }\n    const samples = Math.max(2, Math.ceil(len / 1));\n    for (let s = 0; s <= samples; s++) {\n      const pt = p.getPointAtLength((s / samples) * len);\n      allPoints.push({ x: pt.x, y: pt.y });\n    }\n    tmpSvg.removeChild(p);\n  }\n  document.body.removeChild(tmpSvg);\n  if (allPoints.length === 0) return 0;\n\n  // Fit into SVG_TRACE_FIT of the canvas.\n  const sx = (GW * SVG_TRACE_FIT) / vbW;\n  const sy = (GH * SVG_TRACE_FIT) / vbH;\n  const s = Math.min(sx, sy);\n  const offsetX = GW * 0.5 - (vbW * s) * 0.5;\n  const offsetY = GH * 0.5 - (vbH * s) * 0.5;\n\n  const scaledPoints = allPoints.map(pt => ({\n    x: offsetX + (pt.x - vbX) * s,\n    y: offsetY + (pt.y - vbY) * s,\n  }));\n\n  // Down-sample to roughly one point per SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS grid cells.\n  const downSampled = [];\n  let lastX = -1e9, lastY = -1e9;\n  for (const pt of scaledPoints) {\n    const dx = pt.x - lastX, dy = pt.y - lastY;\n    if (dx * dx + dy * dy >= SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS * SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS) {\n      downSampled.push(pt);\n      lastX = pt.x; lastY = pt.y;\n    }\n  }\n\n  svgTraceState = {\n    points: downSampled,\n    idx: 0,\n    pigment: currentPigment,\n    brushPx: SVG_TRACE_BRUSH_PX,\n    strength: SVG_TRACE_STRENGTH,\n  };\n  markCanvasActive();\n  return downSampled.length;\n}\n\nfunction svgTraceStep() {\n  if (!svgTraceState) return;\n  const st = svgTraceState;\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  for (let i = 0; i < SVG_POINTS_PER_FRAME && st.idx < st.points.length; i++, st.idx++) {\n    const pt = st.points[st.idx];\n    paintAt(pt.x, pt.y, st.brushPx * pxToGridRadius, st.pigment, st.strength);\n  }\n  if (st.idx >= st.points.length) svgTraceState = null;\n}\n\nfunction cancelSVGTrace() { svgTraceState = null; }\n\n// Apply the default paper-wetness preset matching Everything below just wires the UI\n// elements that already exist in the page to method calls on\n// a single Watercolor instance.\n//\n// Layout: ~400 lines of wiring, organized in the same order the\n// UI is presented in the panel.\n\n// ----- Create the instance ----------------------------------------------\n// Pass the .app-section as the target. The lib appends its <canvas> there\n// (the old inline <canvas id=\"canvas\"> was replaced with a #canvas-host\n// div above). canvasScale: 1 \u2014 full-viewport canvas matches the original\n// brush tuning so animations look the same as in v0.26.2.\nconst host = document.getElementById('canvas-host');\nhost.style.cssText = 'position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;';\nconst wc = Watercolor.create(host, { canvasScale: 1 });\nwindow.Watercolor = wc;  // expose for console use (replaces v0.26.2's window.Watercolor)\n\n// ----- Initial paint so the canvas isn't blank --------------------------\nconst w0 = wc.grid.width, h0 = wc.grid.height;\nwc.paintAt(w0 * 0.40, h0 * 0.50, w0 * 0.04, 0, 0.7);\nwc.paintAt(w0 * 0.60, h0 * 0.50, w0 * 0.04, 2, 0.7);\n\n// ----- Pigment swatches -------------------------------------------------\n// v0.26.2 generated swatches dynamically in JS. v0.27 uses static\n// <div class=\"swatch\"> elements that already exist in #pigments and\n// #special-brushes. We tag them with data-pigment, then wire clicks.\n//\n// Pigment indices: 0=rose, 1=yellow, 2=blue, -1=water, -2=lift,\n// -3=rainbow, -4=mask. The DOM order in the panel matches these.\nconst pigmentsContainer = document.getElementById('pigments');\nconst specialContainer = document.getElementById('special-brushes');\nconst swatchEls = [];\n\n// Generate swatches if the containers are empty (v0.26.2's HTML had\n// them as empty divs that JS filled in).\nfunction buildSwatches() {\n  // Standard 3 pigments\n  const standard = [\n    { idx: 0, name: 'Quinacridone Rose',  bg: '#c95e84' },\n    { idx: 1, name: 'Hansa Yellow',       bg: '#e8c44a' },\n    { idx: 2, name: 'Cerulean Blue',      bg: '#5089b0' },\n  ];\n  pigmentsContainer.innerHTML = '';\n  for (const p of standard) {\n    const el = document.createElement('button');\n    el.className = 'swatch';\n    el.dataset.pigment = String(p.idx);\n    el.title = p.name;\n    el.style.background = p.bg;\n    pigmentsContainer.appendChild(el);\n    swatchEls.push(el);\n  }\n\n  // Special brushes\n  const special = [\n    { idx: -1, name: 'Water',   cls: 'water' },\n    { idx: -2, name: 'Lift',    cls: 'lift' },\n    { idx: -3, name: 'Rainbow', cls: 'rainbow' },\n    { idx: -4, name: 'Mask',    cls: 'mask' },\n  ];\n  specialContainer.innerHTML = '';\n  for (const p of special) {\n    const el = document.createElement('button');\n    el.className = 'swatch swatch-' + p.cls;\n    el.dataset.pigment = String(p.idx);\n    el.title = p.name;\n    // Approximate styling \u2014 final visual matches what v0.26.2 generated.\n    if (p.idx === -1) {\n      el.style.background = 'rgba(180, 210, 220, 0.25)';\n      el.style.border = '2px solid rgba(110, 145, 160, 0.65)';\n    } else if (p.idx === -2) {\n      el.style.background = 'rgba(245, 240, 232, 0.20)';\n      el.style.border = '2px dashed rgba(95, 85, 75, 0.78)';\n    } else if 74, 0.45)';\n      el.style.border = '2px dashed rgba(180, 140, 30, 0.7)';\n    }\n    specialContainer.appendChild(el);\n    swatchEls.push(el);\n  }\n}\nbuildSwatches();\n\n// Mark the initial pigment as active and wire clicks\nfunction highlightActiveSwatch() {\n  const active = wc.pigment();\n  for (const el of swatchEls) {\n    el.classList.toggle('active', parseInt(el.dataset.pigment, 10) === active);\n  }\n}\nswatchEls.forEach(el => {\n  el.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    const idx = parseInt(el.dataset.pigment, 10);\n    wc.pigment(idx);\n    highlightActiveSwatch();\n    updateCursorPreview();\n  });\n});\nhighlightActiveSwatch();\n\n// ----- Sliders -----------------------------------------------------------\nfunction wireSlider(id, valueId, apiFn, fmt) {\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\n  const out = document.getElementById(valueId);\n  if (!el) return;\n  el.addEventListener('input', () => {\n    const v = parseFloat(el.value);\n    apiFn(v);\n    if (out) out.textContent = fmt ? fmt(v) : v;\n    updateCursorPreview();\n  });\n  // Initial sync\n  el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));\n}\nwireSlider('size',       'size-value',       v => wc.brushSize(v));\nwireSlider('paint-load', 'paint-load-value', v => wc.paintLoad(v), v => v.toFixed(1) + '\u00d7');\nwireSlider('water-load', 'water-load-value', v => wc.waterLoad(v), v => v.toFixed(1) + '\u00d7');\n// fade-halflife \u2014 original used setFadeHalfLifeMs which isn't exposed in\n// the lib yet. Leave the slider visible but inert for now; future v2\n// addition can hook it up.\n\n// ----- Paper-wetness preset ---------------------------------------------\nconst evapSelect = document.getElementById('evap-preset');\nif (evapSelect) {\n  evapSelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n    wc.paperWetness(evapSelect.value);\n  });\n  // v0.26.2 defaulted to wetOnWet \u2014 lib does the same automatically.\n}\n\n// ----- Scale slider ------------------------------------------------------\n// SCALE is fixed at create-time in lib v2. Hide the slider for v0.27.\nconst scaleSlider = document.getElementById('scale-slider');\nif (scaleSlider && scaleSlider.parentNode) {\n  scaleSlider.parentNode.style.display = 'none';\n}\n\n// ----- Animation / visualization / background ---------------------------\nconst animationSelect = document.getElementById('animation-mode');\nif (animationSelect) {\n  animationSelect.addEventListener('change', () => wc.setAnimation(animationSelect.value || 'off'));\n}\nconst vizSelect = document.getElementById('visualization-mode');\nif (vizSelect) {\n  vizSelect.addEventListener('change', () => wc.setVisualization(vizSelect.value || 'off'));\n}\nconst timeOfDaySelect = document.getElementById('time-of-day');\nif (timeOfDaySelect) {\n  timeOfDaySelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n    if (timeOfDaySelect.value) wc.setBackground(timeOfDaySelect.value);\n  });\n}\n\n// ----- Action buttons ----------------------------------------------------\nfunction flash(el) {\n  if (!el) return;\n  el.classList.add('on');\n  setTimeout(() => el.classList.remove('on'), 220);\n}\nfunction wireBtn(id, fn) {\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\n  if (!el) return;\n  el.addEventListener('click', () => { fn(); flash(el); });\n}\nwireBtn('rewet-btn',       () => wc.rewet());\nwireBtn('big-splash-btn',  () => wc.splash('bigSplash'));\nwireBtn('fine-spritz-btn', () => wc.splash('fineSpritz'));\nwireBtn('dry-btn',         () => wc.dry());\nwireBtn('remove-mask-btn', () => wc.removeMask());\nwireBtn('reset',           () => wc.reset());\nwireBtn('export-btn', () => {\n  const url = wc.exportPNG();\n  const a = document.createElement('a');\n  a.href = url;\n  const stamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);\n  a.download = 'watercolor-' + stamp + '.png';\n  a.click();\n});\n\n// ----- Toggles -----------------------------------------------------------\nfunction wireToggle(id, getter, setter) {\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\n  if (!el) return;\n  function sync() {\n    const v = !!getter();\n    el.classList.toggle('on', v);\n    el.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(v));\n  }\n  sync();\n  el.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    setter(!getter());\n    sync();\n  });\n}\nwireToggle('toggle-edge',     () => wc.edgeDarkening(),     v => wc.edgeDarkening(v));\nwireToggle('toggle-fade',     () => wc.fadePainting(),      v => wc.fadePainting(v));\nwireToggle('toggle-drying',   () => wc.pauseDrying(),       v => wc.pauseDrying(v));\nwireToggle('toggle-autodry',  () => wc.autoDryBackground(), v => wc.autoDryBackground(v));\nwireToggle('toggle-transparent', () => wc.transparent(),    v => {\n  wc.transparent(v);\n  document.body.classList.toggle('transparent-canvas', v);\n});\n\n// WebGL toggle: not supported in lib v2 yet \u2014 disable visually.\nconst webglToggle = document.getElementById('toggle-webgl');\nif (webglToggle) {\n  webglToggle.disabled = true;\n  webglToggle.style.opacity = '0.4';\n  webglToggle.title = 'WebGL render path not yet ported to library (v2 will add)';\n}\n\n// ----- Paper color picker -----------------------------------------------\nconst paperColorEl = document.getElementById('paper-color');\nif (paperColorEl) {\n  // Initialize from library\n  const c = wc.paperColor();\n  const toHex = (f) => Math.round(f * 255).toString(16).padStart(2, '0');\n  paperColorEl.value = '#' + toHex(c.r) + toHex(c.g) + toHex(c.b);\n  paperColorEl.addEventListener('input', () => {\n    const hex = paperColorEl.value.replace(/^#/, '');\n    const r = parseInt(hex.substr(0, 2), 16) / 255;\n    const g = parseInt(hex.substr(2, 2), 16) / 255;\n    const b = parseInt(hex.substr(4, 2), 16) / 255;\n    wc.paperColor(r, g, b);\n    // Also update the page body background to match (matches v0.26.2).\n    document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--canvas-bg', paperColorEl.value);\n  });\n}\n\n// ----- Text painting ----------------------------------------------------\nconst textField = document.getElementById('text-input');\nconst textBtn   = document.getElementById('text-paint-btn');\nfunction doTextPaint() {\n  if (!textField) return;\n  const t = textField.value;\n  if (!t) return;\n  wc.paintText(t);\n  flash(textBtn);\n}\nif (textBtn) textBtn.addEventListener('click', doTextPaint);\nif (textField) textField.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n  if (e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); doTextPaint(); }\n});\n\n// ----- SVG tracing ------------------------------------------------------\nconst svgFileInput = document.getElementById('svg-file-input');\nconst svgLoadBtn   = document.getElementById('svg-load-btn');\nif (svgLoadBtn && svgFileInput) {\n  svgLoadBtn.addEventListener('click', () => svgFileInput.click());\n  svgFileInput.addEventListener('change', async (e) => {\n    const file = e.target.files && e.target.files[0];\n    if (!file) return;\n    const text = await file.text();\n    try {\n      wc.traceSVG(text);\n      flash(svgLoadBtn);\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.error('SVG trace failed:', err);\n    }\n    svgFileInput.value = '';  // allow re-loading same file\n  });\n}\n\n// ----- Cursor preview ---------------------------------------------------\n// Re-implements v0.26.2's floating cursor preview using the library's\n// brushSize + pigment APIs. The element exists in HTML as #cursor.\nconst cursorEl = document.getElementById('cursor');\nlet _cursorVisible = false;\nfunction updateCursorPreview() {\n  if (!cursorEl) return;\n  const size = wc.brushSize();\n  cursorEl.style.width  = size + 'px';\n  cursorEl.style.height = size + 'px';\n  const pig = wc.pigment();\n  // Style based on pigment. v0.26.2 had matching applyCursorColor logic.\n  cursorEl.style.borderStyle = 'solid';\n  if (pig === -1) {\n    // water\n    cursorEl.style.background = 'rgba(180, 210, 220, 0.18)';\n    cursorEl.style.border = '1.5px solid rgba(110, 145, 160, 0.65)';\n  } else if (pig === -2) {\n    // lift\n    cursorEl.style.background = 'rgba(245, 240, 232, 0.22)';\n    cursorEl.style.border = '1.5px dashed rgba(95, 85, 75, 0.78)';\n  } else if (pig === -3) {\n    // rainbow \u2014 color updated each frame by the loop below\n    cursorEl.style.border = '1.5px solid rgba(95, 85, 75, 0.55)';\n  } else if (pig === -4) {\n    // mask\n    cursorEl.style.background = 'rgba(232, 196, 74, 0.40)';\n    cursorEl.style.border = '1.5px dashed rgba(180, 140, 30, 0.78)';\n  } else {\n    // standard pigment \u2014 use swatch color\n    const colors = ['#c95e84', '#e8c44a', '#5089b0'];\n    cursorEl.style.background = colors[pig] || '#c95e84';\n    cursorEl.style.border = '1.5px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.30)';\n  }\n}\nfunction tickRainbowCursor() {\n  if (!cursorEl) return;\n  if (wc.pigment() === -3) {\n    const c = wc.rainbowColor();\n    const r = Math.round(c.r * 255 + 100);   // brighten \u2014 Sx are reflectance contributions\n    const g = Math.round(c.g * 255 + 100);\n    const b = Math.round(c.b * 255 + 100);\n    cursorEl.style.background = 'rgba(' + r + ',' + g + ',' + b + ',0.40)';\n  }\n  requestAnimationFrame(tickRainbowCursor);\n}\ntickRainbowCursor();\nupdateCursorPreview();\n\ndocument.addEventListener('pointermove', (e) => {\n  if (!cursorEl) return;\n  cursorEl.style.left = e.clientX + 'px';\n  cursorEl.style.top  = e.clientY + 'px';\n  if (!_cursorVisible) {\n    cursorEl.style.opacity = '1';\n    _cursorVisible = true;\n  }\n});\ndocument.addEventListener('pointerleave', () => {\n  if (cursorEl) cursorEl.style.opacity = '0';\n  _cursorVisible = false;\n});\n\n// ----- UI visibility toggle ---------------------------------------------\nconst uiToggle = document.getElementById('ui-toggle');\nif (uiToggle) {\n  let hidden = false;\n  function syncUI() {\n    document.body.classList.toggle('ui-hidden', hidden);\n    uiToggle.textContent = hidden ? '+' : '\u2212';\n    uiToggle.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(hidden));\n    uiToggle.setAttribute('aria-label', hidden ? 'Show controls' : 'Hide controls');\n  }\n  uiToggle.addEventListener('click', () => { hidden = !hidden; syncUI(); });\n  // 'H' shortcut to match v0.26.2\n  document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n    if (e.target && (e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || e.target.tagName === 'TEXTAREA')) return;\n    if (e.key === 'h' || e.key === 'H') { hidden = !hidden; syncUI(); }\n  });\n}\n\n// ----- Perf overlay -----------------------------------------------------\n// Lib v2 doesn't expose perf metrics yet. Hide the overlay if it exists.\nconst perfOverlay = document.getElementById('perf-overlay');\nif (perfOverlay) perfOverlay.hidden = true;\n\n// ----- Dryness indicator + pigment readout ------------------------------\n// Poll the lib's state() every ~250ms to update the bottom bar and the\n// pigment readout label. '100%' : '30%';\n}\nsetInterval(updateReadouts, 250);\nupdateReadouts();\n\n// ----- Console hint -----------------------------------------------------\nconsole.log(\n  '%cWatercolor v0.27 %c\u00b7 lib v2 \u00b7 %c' + Object.keys(wc).filter(k => typeof wc[k] === 'function').length + ' methods on window.Watercolor',\n  'color:#d4a373;font-weight:bold', 'color:#888', 'color:#888'\n);\n",

[\\n    { idx: 0, name: 'Quinacridone Rose',  bg: '#c95e84' },\\n    { idx: 1, name: 'Hansa Yellow',       bg: '#e8c44a' },\\n    { idx: 2, name: 'Cerulean Blue',      bg: '#5089b0' },\\n  ];\\n  pigmentsContainer.innerHTML = '';\\n  for (const p of standard) {\\n    const el = document.createElement('button');\\n    el.className = 'swatch';\\n    el.dataset.pigment = String(p.idx);\\n    el.title = p.name;\\n    el.style.background = p.bg;\\n    pigmentsContainer.appendChild(el);\\n    swatchEls.push(el);\\n  }\\n\\n  // Special brushes\\n  const special = [\\n    { idx: -1, name: 'Water',   cls: 'water' },\\n    { idx: -2, name: 'Lift',    cls: 'lift' },\\n    { idx: -3, name: 'Rainbow', cls: 'rainbow' },\\n    { idx: -4, name: 'Mask',    cls: 'mask' },\\n  ];\\n  specialContainer.innerHTML = '';\\n  for (const p of special) {\\n    const el = document.createElement('button');\\n    el.className = 'swatch swatch-' + p.cls;\\n    el.dataset.pigment = String(p.idx);\\n    el.title = p.name;\\n    // Approximate styling \\u2014 final visual matches what v0.26.2 generated.\\n    if (p.idx === -1) {\\n      el.style.background = 'rgba(180, 210, 220, 0.25)';\\n      el.style.border = '2px solid rgba(110, 145, 160, 0.65)';\\n    } else if (p.idx === -2) {\\n      el.style.background = 'rgba(245, 240, 232, 0.20)';\\n      el.style.border = '2px -4) {\\n      el.style.background = 'rgba(232, 196, 74, 0.45)';\\n      el.style.border = '2px dashed rgba(180, 140, 30, 0.7)';\\n    }\\n    specialContainer.appendChild(el);\\n    swatchEls.push(el);\\n  }\\n}\\nbuildSwatches();\\n\\n// Mark the initial pigment as active and wire clicks\\nfunction highlightActiveSwatch() {\\n  const active = wc.pigment();\\n  for (const el of swatchEls) {\\n    el.classList.toggle('active', parseInt(el.dataset.pigment, 10) === active);\\n  }\\n}\\nswatchEls.forEach(el => {\\n  el.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n    const idx = parseInt(el.dataset.pigment, 10);\\n    wc.pigment(idx);\\n    highlightActiveSwatch();\\n    updateCursorPreview();\\n  });\\n});\\nhighlightActiveSwatch();\\n\\n// ----- Sliders -----------------------------------------------------------\\nfunction wireSlider(id, valueId, apiFn, fmt) {\\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\\n  const out = document.getElementById(valueId);\\n  if (!el) return;\\n  el.addEventListener('input', () => {\\n    const v = parseFloat(el.value);\\n    apiFn(v);\\n    if (out) out.textContent = fmt ? fmt(v) : v;\\n    updateCursorPreview();\\n  });\\n  // Initial sync\\n  el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));\\n}\\nwireSlider('size',       'size-value',       v => v.toFixed(1) + '\\u00d7');\\n// fade-halflife \\u2014 original used setFadeHalfLifeMs which isn't exposed in\\n// the lib yet. Leave the slider visible but inert for now; future v2\\n// addition can hook it up.\\n\\n// ----- Paper-wetness preset ---------------------------------------------\\nconst evapSelect = document.getElementById('evap-preset');\\nif (evapSelect) {\\n  evapSelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\\n    wc.paperWetness(evapSelect.value);\\n  });\\n  // v0.26.2 defaulted to wetOnWet \\u2014 lib does the same automatically.\\n}\\n\\n// ----- Scale slider ------------------------------------------------------\\n// SCALE is fixed at create-time in lib v2. Hide the slider for v0.27.\\nconst scaleSlider = document.getElementById('scale-slider');\\nif (scaleSlider && scaleSlider.parentNode) {\\n  scaleSlider.parentNode.style.display = 'none';\\n}\\n\\n// ----- Animation / visualization / background ---------------------------\\nconst animationSelect = document.getElementById('animation-mode');\\nif (animationSelect) {\\n  animationSelect.addEventListener('change', () => wc.setAnimation(animationSelect.value || 'off'));\\n}\\nconst vizSelect = document.getElementById('visualization-mode');\\nif (vizSelect) {\\n  vizSelect.addEventListener('change', () => wc.setVisualization(vizSelect.value || 'off'));\\n}\\nconst timeOfDaySelect = document.getElementById('time-of-day');\\nif (timeOfDaySelect) {\\n  timeOfDaySelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\\n    if (timeOfDaySelect.value) wc.setBackground(timeOfDaySelect.value);\\n  });\\n}\\n\\n// ----- Action buttons ----------------------------------------------------\\nfunction flash(el) {\\n  if (!el) return;\\n  el.classList.add('on');\\n  setTimeout(() => el.classList.remove('on'), 220);\\n}\\nfunction wireBtn(id, fn) {\\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\\n  if (!el) return;\\n  el.addEventListener('click', () => { fn(); flash(el); });\\n}\\nwireBtn('rewet-btn',       () => wc.rewet());\\nwireBtn('big-splash-btn',  () => wc.splash('bigSplash'));\\nwireBtn('fine-spritz-btn', () => wc.splash('fineSpritz'));\\nwireBtn('dry-btn',         () => wc.dry());\\nwireBtn('remove-mask-btn', () => wc.removeMask());\\nwireBtn('reset',           () => wc.reset());\\nwireBtn('export-btn', () => {\\n  const url = wc.exportPNG();\\n  const a = a.click();\\n});\\n\\n// ----- Toggles -----------------------------------------------------------\\nfunction wireToggle(id, getter, setter) {\\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\\n  if (!el) {\\n    setter(!getter());\\n    sync();\\n  });\\n}\\nwireToggle('toggle-edge',     () => wc.edgeDarkening(),     v => wc.edgeDarkening(v));\\nwireToggle('toggle-fade',     () => wc.fadePainting(),   // brighten \\u2014 Sx are reflectance contributions\\n    const g = Math.round(c.g * 255 + 100);\\n    const b = Math.round(c.b * 255 + 100);\\n    cursorEl.style.background = 'rgba(' + r + ',' + g + ',' + b + ',0.40)';\\n  }\\n  requestAnimationFrame(tickRainbowCursor);\\n}\\ntickRainbowCursor();\\nupdateCursorPreview();\\n\\ndocument.addEventListener('pointermove', (e) => {\\n  if (!cursorEl) return;\\n  cursorEl.style.left = e.clientX + 'px';\\n  cursorEl.style.top  = e.clientY + 'px';\\n  if (!_cursorVisible) {\\n    cursorEl.style.opacity = '1';\\n    _cursorVisible = true;\\n  }\\n});\\ndocument.addEventListener('pointerleave', () => {\\n  if (cursorEl) cursorEl.style.opacity = '0';\\n  _cursorVisible = false;\\n});\\n\\n// ----- UI visibility toggle ---------------------------------------------\\nconst uiToggle = document.getElementById('ui-toggle');\\nif (uiToggle) {\\n  let hidden = false;\\n  function syncUI() {\\n    document.body.classList.toggle('ui-hidden', hidden);\\n    uiToggle.textContent = hidden ? sd.data[j+1] = 0; sd.data[j+2] = 0; sd.data[j+3] = 0;\\n          continue;\\n        }\\n        const speckle = 0.92 + 0.12 * smoothNoise(x * 0.3, y * 0.3);\\n        // Wet paper looks slightly darker and a touch cooler than dry paper.\\n        // Use the same paper base color so the swatch matches its surroundings,\\n        // then nudge it toward blue-gray.\\n        const grad = 0.94 - (y / 56) * 0.04;     // mild top-to-bottom shading\\n        const R = PAPER_R_BASE * speckle * grad * 0.94;\\n        const G = PAPER_G_BASE * speckle * grad * 0.96;\\n        const B = PAPER_B_BASE * speckle * grad * 1.02;\\n        sd.data[j]     = Math.min(255, R * 255);\\n        sd.data[j + 1] = Math.min(255, G * 255);\\n        sd.data[j + 2] = Math.min(255, B * 255);\\n        sd.data[j + 3] = 255;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    sctx.putImageData(sd, 0, 0);\\n    swatchDiv.appendChild(sc);\\n\\n    const lbl = document.createElement('div');\\n    lbl.className = 'pigment-label';\\n    lbl.innerHTML = 'Water';\\n\\n    wrap.appendChild(swatchDiv);\\n    wrap.appendChild(lbl);\\n\\n    wrap.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n      currentPigment = WATER_INDEX;\\n      document.querySelectorAll('.pigment').forEach(el => el.classList.remove('active'));\\n      wrap.classList.add('active');\\n      applyCursorColor();\\n    });\\n\\n    root.appendChild(wrap);\\n  }\\n\\n  // Lift swatch \\u2014 subtractive brush. Rendered as paper with a slightly\\n  // brighter center (the \\\"absorbed spot\\\" left by a tissue blot) and a\\n  // gentle warm tint, so it visually contrasts the water swatch's cool\\n  // tint and reads as removal rather than addition.\\n  {\\n    const wrap = document.createElement('div');\\n    wrap.className = 'pigment' + (currentPigment === LIFT_INDEX ? ' active' : '');\\n    wrap.title = 'Lift \\u2014 removes pigment from the paper, water level unchanged';\\n    wrap.dataset.idx = LIFT_INDEX;\\n\\n    const swatchDiv = document.createElement('div');\\n    swatchDiv.className = 'pigment-swatch';\\n\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor.html\"}"
    }

    "type": "text",
    "text": "**Two real bugs fixed.**\n\n**Why the swatches looked different:** v0.26.2's `buildPigmentSwatches()` doesn't make CSS buttons \u2014 it generates 56\u00d756 `<canvas>` elements and renders each one through the **actual KM compositor** (`kmReflect`) over the actual paper-texture noise field. That's why they have:\n- Visible paper grain (`smoothNoise` per pixel)\n- Top-to-bottom thickness gradient (thicker at the bottom)\n- Edge fade (transparent outside the swatch circle)\n- True pigment color from the K/S triplets, not approximate CSS hex\n- The water swatch has subtle wet-paper cool tint\n- The lift swatch has a brighter \"blotted\" center\n- The mask swatch picks up the amber tint\n\nMy v0.27 made flat CSS buttons because I didn't extract the real generator. Now extracted into the lib as `wc.buildPigmentSwatches(rootEl)` \u2014 same 326 lines from v0.26.2, parameterized to take a root element. The wiring just calls it; swatches manage their own active state and dispatch a `pigmentchange` event that the wiring listens to for the cursor preview.\n\nThe swatches now rebuild whenever paper color changes (matching v0.26.2), so the substrate visible under each pigment updates with the picker.\n\n**Why performance was much worse \u2014 the real bug:** My main loop incremented only `framesSincePaint++`. v0.26.2 increments **three** counters: `framesSincePaint`, `framesSinceIdleCheck`, `framesSinceFade`.\n\nWithout `framesSinceIdleCheck++`, the idle-skip gate in `shouldRunSim()` was permanently stuck:\n\n```js\nif (framesSinceIdleCheck < IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL) return true;  // 0 < 30 forever\n```\n\n`shouldRunSim()` returned `true` every frame. The sim **never** entered its idle-skip path. v0.26.2 normally drops to ~0 CPU when the canvas is settled (no painting, no animation, no wet pigment moving); v0.27 was running `simStep()` + `render()` every frame regardless. That's a 60\u00d7/sec full simulation pass over potentially 1.5M cells, where v0.26.2 would do approximately nothing.\n\nFixed: all three counters increment every frame, same as v0.26.2. Now `shouldRunSim()` works correctly \u2014 every 30th frame it checks `simIsIdle`, and if the canvas is settled, the rest of the frames just skip sim+render entirely. Should restore the original responsiveness.\n\nBoth v0.27 and the library are updated. The swatches should match v0.26.2's exactly, and performance should be back to what you're used to.",
    "citations": []
  }
]

Fixed those, then exposed via `inst.scale(v)`. Verified in the test:\n\n- `scale=1.75` \u2192 900\u00d7600 grid (default)\n- `scale=3` \u2192 525\u00d7350 (lower res, faster sim)\n- `scale=1` \u2192 1575\u00d71050 (max detail, more cells)\n- Bad values rejected with clear error\n\nThe wiring uses **80ms debounce** on input events \u2014 dragging the slider fires ~30 events/sec, and each call to `scale()` reallocates ~12 typed arrays totaling several MB. Debouncing means only the final value triggers the rebuild.\n\nThe lib dispatches a `'rescaled'` CustomEvent on `targetEl` after rebuilding, so the wiring re-runs `rebuildSwatches()` to render them against the new paper texture.\n\n**Fade half-life slider:** `setFadeHalfLifeMs()` was already in the lib but unexposed. Added `inst.fadeHalfLife(ms)`:\n\n- Default: 4000ms (4-second half-life)\n- Range: 500ms..30000ms (clamped automatically)\n- Updates the `fadeFactor` derived from `Math.pow(0.5, FADE_TICK_INTERVAL / 60 / (ms / 1000))`\n\nWired in v0.27 with immediate response on every input event (no debounce needed \u2014 it just adjusts one number).\n\nBoth sliders now respond exactly as they did in v0.26.2. The fade slider only has visible effect when **Fade painting** toggle is on; the resolution slider's effect is immediate (canvas momentarily clears to wet paper, then you can paint at the new detail level).",

The input handler calls `updateCursorPreview()`, which references `cursorEl`. But the cursor preview block was 200 lines further down. Reading a `const` before its declaration line = TDZ error.\n\nFixed by moving the entire cursor-preview block (declaration + functions + listeners) above the swatches and sliders, so `cursorEl` and `updateCursorPreview` exist by the time anything else tries to use them.\n\n**3. WebGL on by default.** Added right after instance creation:\n\n```js\nif (wc.webglAvailable()) {\n  wc.webgl(true);\n}\n```\n\nLazy-init happens on the first `webglAvailable()` call \u2014 if WebGL2 is supported, the next line flips on the GPU path. If not, the toggle stays disabled (greyed out) and the CPU path keeps running, same as before.\n\nThe toggle's initial UI state now reflects reality \u2014 it'll show as **on** (with the accent border) when you load v0.27, matching the actual render path.",

The host UI (v0.27's overlay) polls this and updates\n// its DOM \u2014 the lib itself doesn't touch perf-related elements.\n//\n// Overhead when off: a single boolean check at each timing point.\n// When on: 4-6 performance.now() calls per frame + Float32Array writes.\nconst PERF_BUFFER_SIZE = 120;          // ~2 sec at 60 fps\nlet perfEnabled = false;\nlet perfFrameIdx = 0;\nconst perfRing = {\n  frame:  new Float32Array(PERF_BUFFER_SIZE),\n  sim:    new Float32Array(PERF_BUFFER_SIZE),\n  render: new Float32Array(PERF_BUFFER_SIZE),\n  extra:  new Float32Array(PERF_BUFFER_SIZE),\n  wash:   new Float32Array(PERF_BUFFER_SIZE),\n};\nlet perfHeapStart = null;\nlet perfLoafCount = 0;\nlet perfLoafObserver = null;\n\nfunction perfActivate(on) {\n  perfEnabled = !!on;\n  if (perfEnabled) {\n    perfFrameIdx = 0;\n    perfLoafCount = 0;\n    if (performance.memory) perfHeapStart = performance.memory.usedJSHeapSize;\n    // Long Animation Frame observer (Chromium-only currently). Counts\n    // frames that blocked the main thread > 50ms.\n    if (!perfLoafObserver && typeof PerformanceObserver !== 'undefined') {\n      try {\n        perfLoafObserver = new PerformanceObserver((list) => {\n          perfLoafCount += list.getEntries().length;\n        });\n        perfLoafObserver.observe({ type: 'long-animation-frame', buffered: true });\n      } catch (e) { /* type not supported on this browser; harmless */ }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfunction perfPercentile(arr, p) {\n  const copy = Array.from(arr);\n  copy.sort((a, b) => a - b);\n  const idx = Math.min(copy.length - 1, Math.floor(p * copy.length));\n  return copy[idx];\n}\nfunction perfMean(arr) {\n  let s = 0;\n  for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) s += arr[i];\n  return s / arr.length;\n}\nfunction perfActiveCells() {\n  if (typeof activeRectMinX === 'undefined' || activeRectMinX > activeRectMaxX) return 0;\n  return (activeRectMaxX - activeRectMinX + 1) *\n         (activeRectMaxY - activeRectMinY + 1);\n}\n\n// Snapshot of current perf state. The host polls this periodically\n// (every ~10 frames is plenty) and renders the values into its overlay.\nfunction perfMetrics() {\n  const f50 = perfPercentile(perfRing.frame, 0.50);\n  const f95 = perfPercentile(perfRing.frame, 0.95);\n  const f99 = perfPercentile(perfRing.frame, 0.99);\n  const fAvg = perfMean(perfRing.frame);\n  const fps = fAvg > 0 ? 1000 / fAvg : 0;\n  const active = perfActiveCells();\n  const totalCells = GW * GH;\n  let heapDeltaMB = null;\n  if (performance.memory && perfHeapStart != null) {\n    heapDeltaMB = (performance.memory.usedJSHeapSize - perfHeapStart) / 1048576;\n  }\n  return {\n    enabled: perfEnabled,\n    fps:        fps,\n    framep50:   f50,\n    framep95:   f95,\n    framep99:   f99,\n    sim:        perfMean(perfRing.sim),\n    render:     perfMean(perfRing.render),\n    extra:      perfMean(perfRing.extra),\n    wash:       perfMean(perfRing.wash),\n    activeCells: active,\n    totalCells:  totalCells,\n    activePct:   totalCells > 0 ? (active / totalCells * 100) : 0,\n    heapDeltaMB: heapDeltaMB,\n    longAnimationFrames: perfLoafObserver ? perfLoafCount : null,\n  };\n}\n\n// ============================================================\n// PER-INSTANCE MAIN LOOP\n// ============================================================\n// Single RAF token per instance. destroy() cancels it.\nlet rafToken = null;\nfunction loop() {\n  // v2 perf instrumentation: gated on perfEnabled. When off the calls\n  // compile down to a single boolean check at each gate \u2014 negligible.\n  const t0 = perfEnabled ? performance.now() : 0;\n\n  applyPendingPaint && applyPendingPaint();\n  if (typeof animationStep === 'function') animationStep();\n  if (typeof visualizationStep === 'function') visualizationStep();\n  if (typeof svgTraceStep === 'function') svgTraceStep();\n  const tExtra = perfEnabled ? performance.now() : 0;\n\n  // All three frame counters must increment every frame. Without\n  // framesSinceIdleCheck++ the idle-skip check at shouldRunSim() is\n  // permanently stuck below IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL and the sim never\n  // enters its idle path \u2014 every frame runs simStep + render even\n  // when the canvas is completely settled. (This was a real bug in\n  // an earlier draft of the lib.)\n  framesSincePaint++;\n  framesSinceIdleCheck++;\n  framesSinceFade++;\n\n  const tBeforeSim = perfEnabled ? performance.now() : 0;\n  const runSim = shouldRunSim();\n  if (runSim) {\n    simStep();\n    simStep();\n  }\n  const tAfterSim = perfEnabled ? performance.now() : 0;\n\n  if (runSim) {\n    // v2 \u2014 route to GPU when useGPU. CPU render() handles its own\n    // forceFull/active-rect logic; gpuRender always does a full pass.\n    if (typeof useGPU !== 'undefined' && useGPU && gpuAvailable) {\n      gpuRender();\n    } else {\n      render();\n    }\n  }\n  const tAfterRender = perfEnabled ? performance.now() : 0;\n\n  // Time-wash animator: each frame, if a time-of-day wash is active,\n  // step its painter forward.\n  if (typeof timeWash !== 'undefined' && timeWash !== null) {\n    timeWashStep && timeWashStep();\n  }\n\n  // Pigment fade\n  if (fadeEnabled && framesSinceFade >= FADE_TICK_INTERVAL) {\n    framesSinceFade = 0;\n    fadeStep();\n  }\n\n  if (perfEnabled) {\n    const tEnd = performance.now();\n    const i = perfFrameIdx++ % PERF_BUFFER_SIZE;\n    perfRing.frame[i]  = tEnd - t0;\n    perfRing.sim[i]    = tAfterSim - tBeforeSim;\n    perfRing.render[i] = tAfterRender - tAfterSim;\n    perfRing.extra[i]  = tExtra - t0;\n    perfRing.wash[i]   = tEnd - tAfterRender;\n  }\n\n  rafToken = requestAnimationFrame(loop);\n}\nrafToken = requestAnimationFrame(loop);",
      "old_str": "// ============================================================\n// PER-INSTANCE MAIN LOOP\n// ============================================================\n// Single RAF token per instance. destroy() cancels it.\nlet rafToken = null;\nfunction loop() {\n  applyPendingPaint && applyPendingPaint();\n  if (typeof animationStep === 'function') animationStep();\n  if (typeof visualizationStep === 'function') visualizationStep();\n  if (typeof svgTraceStep === 'function') svgTraceStep();\n\n  // All three frame counters must increment every frame. Without\n  // framesSinceIdleCheck++ the idle-skip check at shouldRunSim() is\n  // permanently stuck below IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL and the sim never\n  // enters its idle path \u2014 every frame runs simStep + render even\n  // when the canvas is completely settled. (This was a real bug in\n  // an earlier draft of the lib.)\n  framesSincePaint++;\n  framesSinceIdleCheck++;\n  framesSinceFade++;\n\n  if (shouldRunSim()) {\n    simStep();\n    simStep();\n    // v2 \u2014 route to GPU when useGPU. String(m.longAnimationFrames) : 'n/a');\n}\nfunction setPerfOn(on) {\n  wc.perf(on);\n  if (perfOverlay) perfOverlay.hidden = !on;\n  if (on) {\n    if (!perfPollTimer) perfPollTimer = setInterval(updatePerfOverlay, 150);\n    updatePerfOverlay();\n  } else {\n    if (perfPollTimer) { clearInterval(perfPollTimer); perfPollTimer = null; }\n  }\n}\nsetPerfOn(false);   // start hidden\n\n// Wire the perf-overlay toggle button. When `perfEnabled` is false, each gate compiles to a single boolean check \u2014 overhead is negligible. When true, 4-6 `performance.now()` calls + ring writes per frame.\n- `perfMetrics()` returns a snapshot object: `{ fps, framep50, framep95, framep99, sim, render, extra, wash, activeCells, totalCells, activePct, heapDeltaMB, longAnimationFrames }`\n\n**API (2 methods):**\n\n```js\nwc.perf()           // get state\nwc.perf(true)       // enable\nwc.perfMetrics()    // current snapshot\n```\n\nThe lib does **not** touch the DOM for perf display \u2014 that's the host UI's job. This page (v0.27) is the same HTML/CSS as v0.26.2, but the inline ~5,800-line <code>&lt;script&gt;</code> block is replaced by <code>&lt;script src=\"watercolor-lib.js\"&gt;&lt;/script&gt;</code> + a ~390-line wiring script that hooks each UI element to a method on the library instance. State isolation is via JavaScript closure semantics &mdash; every variable inside the factory's <code>create()</code> call is unique per instance.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> The library can also be used to mount multiple independent watercolor instances on one page: <code>Watercolor.create(targetEl)</code> returns a fresh handle whose state is fully isolated from other instances. See <code>watercolor-multi-example.html</code> and <code>watercolor-simple-example.html</code> alongside the library.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> Auto canvas-scale: brushes from animations and visualizations stay visually proportional on small canvases. A 320px-wide embedded canvas auto-downscales brush radii by ~0.27&times; relative to the standalone tuning. Override per-instance via <code>{ canvasScale: 1.0 }</code> in the create options.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> WebGL render path on by default when available (was opt-in via toggle in v0.20&ndash;v0.26.2). Falls back silently to CPU on browsers without WebGL2.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> New <strong>Perf</strong> toggle in the toggles row. Same overlay as v0.19 (FPS, p50/p95/p99 frame times, per-phase ms, active cells, heap delta, Long Animation Frames). Library exposes <code>perfMetrics()</code> as a snapshot method; UI polls it every 150ms.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> Brush sizes in animations + splash were tuned for a ~1200px-wide canvas and looked disproportionate on smaller hosts. Fixed by the auto canvas-scale above.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> The pigment swatches in v0.27's first build were flat CSS buttons; v0.26.2 used real KM-compositor-rendered <code>&lt;canvas&gt;</code> swatches (paper texture, edge fade, granulation). Extracted the original <code>buildPigmentSwatches()</code> as <code>inst.buildPigmentSwatches(rootEl)</code>; the wiring calls it and the swatches now look identical to v0.26.2.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> Major perf bug in the first library draft: only <code>framesSincePaint</code> incremented per frame, not <code>framesSinceIdleCheck</code> or <code>framesSinceFade</code>. The idle-skip gate in <code>shouldRunSim()</code> was permanently stuck at 0 &lt; 30, so the sim never skipped frames even on settled canvases. Lib now matches v0.26.2's three-counter loop exactly.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> Main loop referenced <code>FADE_INTERVAL_FRAMES</code> and <code>applyPigmentFade</code> &mdash; neither exists. Real names are <code>FADE_TICK_INTERVAL</code> and <code>fadeStep</code>. Without this, fade silently threw every frame when fade was on.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> Cursor preview TDZ error: <code>wireSlider</code> fires an initial input event to sync the readout, which called <code>updateCursorPreview()</code>, which referenced <code>cursorEl</code> &mdash; declared later in the script. Moved cursor preview setup ahead of the swatches and sliders.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.26 ===== -->\n        <h2 id=\"cl-0-26\">v0.26 &mdash; Multi-instance bridge (deprecated)</h2>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> First attempt at multi-instance support via a <code>postMessage</code> bridge: each iframe loaded a standalone Watercolor and the parent page called methods over the postMessage protocol. Worked in principle, fragile in practice &mdash; <code>file://</code> origin restrictions varied between Chrome / Firefox / Safari and the bridge often hung silently.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>v0.26.1</strong> added belt-and-suspenders ready signaling: <code>wc:ready</code> announced on script eval, again on next tick, and on receipt of a parent ping. Reply targetOrigin set to <code>'*'</code> instead of echoing <code>e.origin</code>.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>v0.26.2</strong> added a <code>body.wc-embedded</code> CSS class auto-applied when running inside an iframe. Collapses the inner control panel, dryness bar, ui-toggle, and docs section &mdash; the parent owns the UI.</li>\n          <li>Superseded by v0.27's library approach. The bridge architecture survives only in <code>watercolor-v0.26.2.html</code> if you specifically need iframe isolation.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.25 ===== -->",

The parent can disable pointer painting per-instance via\n  4343\t// options.pointer === false.\n  4344\t\n  4345\tconst _pointerEnabled = options.pointer !== false;\n  4346\tlet _isPainting = false;\n  4347\tlet _lastGX = 0, _lastGY = 0;\n  4348\tlet _pendingPaintX = null, _pendingPaintY = null;\n  4349\t\n  4350\tfunction _clientToGrid(e) {\n  4351\t  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  4352\t  const cx = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;\n  4353\t  const cy = (e.clientY - rect.top)  / rect.height;\n  4354\t  return { gx: cx * GW, gy: cy * GH };\n  4355\t}\n  4356\t\n  4357\tfunction _onPointerDown(e) {\n  4358\t  if (!_pointerEnabled) return;\n  4359\t  e.preventDefault();\n  4360\t  _isPainting = true;\n  4361\t  const { gx, gy } = _clientToGrid(e);\n  4362\t  // brushSize is in display pixels \u2014 convert to grid radius. Halve for\n  4363\t  // radius (vs diameter), divide by SCALE for grid units.\n  4364\t  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  4365\t  paintAt(gx, gy, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  4366\t  _lastGX = gx; _lastGY = gy;\n  4367\t  canvas.setPointerCapture && canvas.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);\n  4368\t}\n  4369\t\n  4370\tfunction _onPointerMove(e) {\n  4371\t  if (!_isPainting || !_pointerEnabled) return;\n  4372\t  e.preventDefault();\n  4373\t  // Queue the latest position; the main loop's applyPendingPaint\n  4374\t  // interpolates from lastGX/lastGY to here, stamping along the path.\n  4375\t  const { gx, gy } = _clientToGrid(e);\n  4376\t  _pendingPaintX = gx;\n  4377\t  _pendingPaintY = gy;\n  4378\t}\n  4379\t\n  4380\tfunction _onPointerUp(e) {\n  4381\t  if (!_isPainting) return;\n  4382\t  _isPainting = false;\n  4383\t  _pendingPaintX = null;\n  4384\t  _pendingPaintY = null;\n  4385\t  canvas.releasePointerCapture && canvas.releasePointerCapture(e.pointerId);\n  4386\t}\n  4387\t\n  4388\t// Interpolated paint: along the path from _lastG to _pendingPaint,\n  4389\t// stamp every ~brushSize/2 pixels worth of distance. Without this, fast\n  4390\t// drags would skip cells and produce broken strokes.\n  4391\tfunction applyPendingPaint() {\n  4392\t  if (_pendingPaintX === null) return;\n  4393\t  const gx = _pendingPaintX, gy = _pendingPaintY;\n  4394\t  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  4395\t  const stampSpacing = Math.max(0.5, r * 0.4);   // grid units between stamps\n  4396\t  const dx = gx - _lastGX, dy = gy - _lastGY;\n  4397\t  const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\n  4398\t  if (dist < stampSpacing) {\n  4399\t    paintAt(gx, gy, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  4400\t  } else {\n  4401\t    const steps = Math.ceil(dist / stampSpacing);\n  4402\t    for (let s = 1; s <= steps; s++) {\n  4403\t      const f = s / steps;\n  4404\t      paintAt(_lastGX + dx * f, _lastGY + dy * f, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  4405\t    }\n  4406\t  }\n  4407\t  _lastGX = gx; _lastGY = gy;\n  4408\t  _pendingPaintX = null;\n  4409\t  _pendingPaintY = null;\n  4410\t}\n  4411\t\n  4412\tif (_pointerEnabled) {\n  4413\t  // Pointer events unify mouse + touch + pen on modern browsers.\n  4414\t  canvas.style.touchAction = 'none';   // prevent scroll on touch drag\n  4415\t  canvas.addEventListener('pointerdown', _onPointerDown);\n  4416\t  canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', _onPointerMove);\n  4417\t  canvas.addEventListener('pointerup',   _onPointerUp);\n  4418\t  canvas.addEventListener('pointercancel', _onPointerUp);\n  4419\t  canvas.addEventListener('pointerleave',  _onPointerUp);\n  4420\t}\n  4421\t\n  4422\t\n  4423\t// ============================================================\n  4424\t// PERFORMANCE INSTRUMENTATION (v2 lib addition \u2014 extracted from v0.19)\n  4425\t// ============================================================\n[4900 lines total]",

The parent can disable pointer painting per-instance via\n// options.pointer === false.\n\nconst _pointerEnabled = options.pointer !== false;\nlet _isPainting = false;\nlet _lastGX = 0, _lastGY = 0;\nlet _pendingPaintX = null, _pendingPaintY = null;\n\nfunction _clientToGrid(e) {\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const cx = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;\n  const cy = (e.clientY - rect.top)  / rect.height;\n  return { gx: cx * GW, gy: cy * GH };\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerDown(e) {\n  if (!_pointerEnabled) return;\n  e.preventDefault();\n  _isPainting = true;\n  const { gx, gy } = _clientToGrid(e);\n  // brushSize is in display pixels \u2014 convert to grid radius. Halve for\n  // radius (vs diameter), divide by SCALE for grid units.\n  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  paintAt(gx, gy, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  _lastGX = gx; _lastGY = gy;\n  canvas.setPointerCapture && canvas.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerMove(e) {\n  if (!_isPainting || !_pointerEnabled) return;\n  e.preventDefault();\n  // Queue the latest position; the main loop's applyPendingPaint\n  // interpolates from lastGX/lastGY to here, stamping along the path.\n  const { gx, gy } = _clientToGrid(e);\n  _pendingPaintX = gx;\n  _pendingPaintY = gy;\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerUp(e) {\n  if (!_isPainting) return;\n  _isPainting = false;\n  _pendingPaintX = null;\n  _pendingPaintY = null;\n  canvas.releasePointerCapture && canvas.releasePointerCapture(e.pointerId);\n}\n\n// Interpolated paint: along the path from _lastG to _pendingPaint,\n// stamp every ~brushSize/2 pixels worth of distance. Without this, fast\n// drags would skip cells and produce broken strokes.\nfunction applyPendingPaint() {\n  if (_pendingPaintX === null) return;\n  const gx = _pendingPaintX, gy = _pendingPaintY;\n  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  const stampSpacing = Math.max(0.5, r * 0.4);   // grid units between stamps\n  const dx = gx - _lastGX, dy = gy - _lastGY;\n  const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\n  if (dist < stampSpacing) {\n    paintAt(gx, gy, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  } else {\n    const steps = Math.ceil(dist / stampSpacing);\n    for (let s = 1; s <= steps; s++) {\n      const f = s / steps;\n      paintAt(_lastGX + dx * f, _lastGY + dy * f, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n    }\n  }\n  _lastGX = gx; _lastGY = gy;\n  _pendingPaintX = null;\n  _pendingPaintY = null;\n}\n\nif (_pointerEnabled) {\n  // Pointer events unify mouse + touch + pen on modern browsers.\n  canvas.style.touchAction = 'none';   // prevent scroll on touch drag\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerdown', _onPointerDown);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', _onPointerMove);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerup',   _onPointerUp);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointercancel', _onPointerUp);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerleave',  _onPointerUp);\n}\n\n\n// ============================================================\n// PERFORMANCE INSTRUMENTATION (v2 lib addition \u2014 extracted from v0.19)\n// ============================================================\n",

      "new_str": "// ============================================================\n// POINTER PAINTING (v2) \u2014 mouse + touch input\n// v0.28 \u2014 adds mobile mode + palm rejection\n// ============================================================\n// Pointer events unify mouse + touch + pen on modern browsers. The\n// per-frame applyPendingPaint() handles interpolation, so fast drags\n// (whether mouse or finger) produce continuous strokes via stamped\n// midpoints rather than dotted lines.\n//\n// v0.28 \u2014 mobile mode. Auto-detected from `(pointer: coarse)` media\n// query but the caller can override via options.mobile, and the host\n// UI can toggle at runtime via inst.mobile(true|false). When mobile\n// mode is on, three palm-rejection heuristics filter accidental\n// contact:\n//   1. CONTACT SIZE \u2014 palms produce wide PointerEvent.width/height.\n//      Touches with width or height > PALM_CONTACT_PX are dropped.\n//   2. PEN LOCKOUT \u2014 once a pen-type pointer is active, any concurrent\n//      touch-type events are palm. Standard iPad-with-Apple-Pencil\n//      pattern: the palm rests while you draw.\n//   3. FIRST-TOUCH-WINS \u2014 once painting starts on pointerId X, ignore\n//      additional touches until the active one ends. Filters\n//      multi-touch palm landings.\n// In mouse / non-mobile mode, none of these gates apply: pointer\n// events fall through as before.\n\n// Detection: matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)') is the standard signal\n// for touch-primary devices. Available in all modern browsers.\nfunction _detectMobile() {\n  if (typeof window === 'undefined' || !window.matchMedia) return false;\n  try {\n    return window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches;\n  } catch (_) {\n    // Fallback for environments without matchMedia\n    return ('ontouchstart' in window) || (navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0);\n  }\n}\n\nconst _mobileDetected = _detectMobile();\nlet _mobileMode = options.mobile !== undefined ? !!options.mobile : _mobileDetected;\nconst PALM_CONTACT_PX = 40;  // contacts wider than this are palms\n\nconst _pointerEnabled = options.pointer !== false;\nlet _isPainting = false;\nlet _lastGX = 0, _lastGY = 0;\nlet _pendingPaintX = null, _pendingPaintY = null;\nlet _activePointerId = null;\nlet _activePointerType = null;\n\nfunction _clientToGrid(e) {\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const cx = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;\n  const cy = (e.clientY - rect.top)  / rect.height;\n  return { gx: cx * GW, gy: cy * GH };\n}\n\n// Returns true if this event should be ignored as a palm contact.\n// Only active when mobile mode is on; otherwise always returns false.\nfunction _isPalmEvent(e) {\n  if (!_mobileMode) return false;\n  // 1. Contact size \u2014 palms produce wide contacts. width/height are in\n  //    CSS pixels; not all browsers populate them (some return 1\u00d71 even\n  //    for real fingers). Treat 1\u00d71 as \"no info, don't filter.\"\n  if (e.pointerType === 'touch' && e.width > 1 && e.height > 1) {\n    if (e.width > PALM_CONTACT_PX || e.height > PALM_CONTACT_PX) return true;\n  }\n  // 2. Pen lockout \u2014 once we're painting with pen, drop touch.\n  if (_activePointerType === 'pen' && e.pointerType === 'touch') return true;\n  // 3. First-touch-wins \u2014 additional touches while painting are palm.\n  if (_isPainting && _activePointerId !== null && e.pointerId !== _activePointerId) {\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerDown(e) {\n  if (!_pointerEnabled) return;\n  if (_isPalmEvent(e)) return;\n  e.preventDefault();\n  _isPainting = true;\n  _activePointerId = e.pointerId;\n  _activePointerType = e.pointerType;\n  const { gx, gy } = _clientToGrid(e);\n  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  paintAt(gx, gy, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  _lastGX = gx; _lastGY = gy;\n  canvas.setPointerCapture && canvas.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerMove(e) {\n  if (!_isPainting || !_pointerEnabled) return;\n  // Only follow the active pointer. Other moves (e.g. palm dragging\n  // while pen draws) get dropped here too.\n  if (_activePointerId !== null && e.pointerId !== _activePointerId) return;\n  if (_isPalmEvent(e)) return;\n  e.preventDefault();\n  const { gx, gy } = _clientToGrid(e);\n  _pendingPaintX = gx;\n  _pendingPaintY = gy;\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerUp(e) {\n  if (!_isPainting) return;\n  // Only end painting when the active pointer ends.\n  if (_activePointerId !== null && e.pointerId !== _activePointerId) return;\n  _isPainting = false;\n  _activePointerId = null;\n  _activePointerType = null;\n  _pendingPaintX = null;\n  _pendingPaintY = null;\n  canvas.releasePointerCapture && canvas.releasePointerCapture(e.pointerId);\n}\n\n// Interpolated paint: along the path from _lastG to _pendingPaint,\n// stamp every ~brushSize/2 pixels worth of distance. Without this, fast\n// drags would skip cells and produce broken strokes.\nfunction applyPendingPaint() {\n  if (_pendingPaintX === null) return;\n  const gx = _pendingPaintX, gy = _pendingPaintY;\n  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  const stampSpacing = Math.max(0.5, r * 0.4);   // grid units between stamps\n  const dx = gx - _lastGX, dy = gy - _lastGY;\n  const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\n  if (dist < stampSpacing) {\n    paintAt(gx, gy, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  } else {\n    const steps = Math.ceil(dist / stampSpacing);\n    for (let s = 1; s <= steps; s++) {\n      const f = s / steps;\n      paintAt(_lastGX + dx * f, _lastGY + dy * f, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n    }\n  }\n  _lastGX = gx; _lastGY = gy;\n  _pendingPaintX = null;\n  _pendingPaintY = null;\n}\n\nif (_pointerEnabled) {\n  canvas.style.touchAction = 'none';   // prevent scroll on touch drag\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerdown', _onPointerDown);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', _onPointerMove);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerup',   _onPointerUp);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointercancel', _onPointerUp);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerleave',  _onPointerUp);\n}",

The button mirrors\n// the lib's state and lets users override regardless of detection \u2014\n// e.g. a developer on a desktop with a touchscreen who wants to test,\n// or someone on an iPad who specifically wants the simpler input model.\n//\n// Side effects: in mobile mode we hide the cursor preview (it tracks\n// pointer position via document-level pointermove, which is redundant\n// with a visible finger) and collapse the control panel by default\n// to give the canvas more screen.\nconst mobileToggleBtn = document.getElementById('toggle-mobile');\nfunction applyMobileUI(on) {\n  // Hide the floating cursor preview when mobile mode is on.\n  if (cursorEl) cursorEl.style.display = on ? 'none' : '';\n  // Tag the body so any CSS can react if desired.\n  document.body.classList.toggle('wc-mobile', on);\n}\nif (mobileToggleBtn) {\n  function syncMobileToggle() {\n    const v = wc.mobile();\n    mobileToggleBtn.classList.toggle('on', v);\n    mobileToggleBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(v));\n  }\n  // Initial sync \u2014 lib auto-enables on touch-primary devices, so the\n  // button comes up pre-pressed on iPad/phone.\n  syncMobileToggle();\n  applyMobileUI(wc.mobile());\n  mobileToggleBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    const next = !wc.mobile();\n    wc.mobile(next);\n    syncMobileToggle();\n    applyMobileUI(next);\n  });\n}\n\n// If mobile mode came up on by auto-detection (touch device on first\n// load), start with the control panel collapsed \u2014 screen real estate\n// is precious on phones, and users can tap the toggle to expand.\nif (wc.mobile()) {\n  const panel = document.querySelector('.controls');\n  if (panel && panel.classList) panel.classList.add('collapsed');\n}",

Auto-enabled on touch-primary devices via <code>matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)')</code> detection at create time; auto-detection result is exposed at <code>inst.mobileDetected()</code> while the live state is <code>inst.mobile()</code>. The toggle is independent of detection, so users on desktop with a touchscreen can opt in, and users on a tablet can opt out.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Long touch strokes.</strong> Already worked at the pointer-event level (the per-frame <code>applyPendingPaint()</code> interpolates from last-known to current with stamp spacing of <code>r &times; 0.4</code>, producing continuous strokes for any drag speed including fast finger swipes). v0.28 ensures touch events get through reliably by handling palm rejection upstream.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Palm rejection (three heuristics, combined).</strong> Active only when mobile mode is on; mouse and pen-only flows are untouched.\n            <ol>\n              <li><strong>Contact size.</strong> <code>PointerEvent.width</code> and <code>height</code> give the contact area in CSS pixels. Touches wider than <code>PALM_CONTACT_PX = 40</code> in either dimension are dropped. (Many browsers report 1&times;1 for touches with no real area information; those bypass this filter to avoid false rejections.)</li>\n              <li><strong>Pen lockout.</strong> Once <code>_activePointerType === 'pen'</code> latches, any concurrent <code>pointerType === 'touch'</code> events are dropped. This is the standard iPad-with-Apple-Pencil pattern: the palm rests on the screen while the stylus draws.</li>\n              <li><strong>First-touch-wins.</strong> When painting starts on <code>pointerId X</code>, the lib remembers X and ignores additional pointer events with different IDs until X ends. Filters multi-touch palm landings on phones, where the palm lands a fraction of a second after the finger.</li>\n            </ol>\n          </li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Mobile-aware UI.</strong> When mobile mode is on, the floating cursor preview is hidden (you can see your finger; the preview was designed for mouse hover) and the body picks up a <code>wc-mobile</code> class so any host CSS can react. On auto-detection, the control panel comes up collapsed to give the canvas more room \u2014 tap the toggle in the corner to expand it.</li>\n          <li>API additions: <code>inst.mobile()</code> / <code>inst.mobile(true|false)</code> getter-setter, <code>inst.mobileDetected()</code> for the one-time detection snapshot.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.27 ===== -->",

    "text": "**v0.28 shipped.** Mobile mode + palm rejection.\n\n**Lib additions (`watercolor-lib.js`):**\n\n- `_detectMobile()` uses `matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)')`, with `ontouchstart`/`maxTouchPoints` fallback for older environments\n- `_mobileDetected` is latched once at create time; `_mobileMode` is the live, runtime-mutable flag\n- `options.mobile` on `Watercolor.create()` overrides auto-detection\n- `_isPalmEvent(e)` runs three filters when mobile mode is on (and only then):\n  1. **Contact size** \u2014 if `e.width > 40 || e.height > 40` on a touch event, reject as palm. The 1\u00d71 sentinel that some browsers report for size-unknown contacts is bypassed to avoid false rejections.\n  2. **Pen lockout** \u2014 once `_activePointerType === 'pen'` latches, concurrent touch events are dropped\n  3. **First-touch-wins** \u2014 once painting starts on `pointerId X`, additional pointers with different IDs are ignored until X ends\n- Two new API methods: `inst.mobile(v)` (getter/setter, returns current state) and `inst.mobileDetected()` (one-time detection result)\n- Mode-toggle clears stroke state if a touch is mid-stroke, so the next event starts cleanly\n\n**v0.28 HTML changes:**\n\n- Title bumped to v0.28\n- New `<button id=\"toggle-mobile\">Mobile</button>` in the toggles row, right after Perf\n- Sidebar nav entry, version subtitle, and footer all updated\n- New changelog entry documenting all three palm-rejection heuristics and the mobile-aware UI changes\n\n**v0.28 wiring (in the HTML):**\n\n- Mobile toggle wired to `wc.mobile(true|false)`, syncs visual `.on` class\n- When mobile mode is on: hides the floating cursor preview (redundant when your finger is visible) and adds `wc-mobile` class to `<body>` so any CSS can react\n- If auto-detection enabled mobile at load (touch device on first visit), the control panel comes up collapsed to give the canvas room \u2014 tap the toggle in the corner to expand\n\n**Verified in Node:**\n\n- Desktop (matchMedia false): `mobileDetected: false`, `mobile: false` initially, toggles cleanly\n- Touch device (matchMedia true): both auto-enabled\n- Explicit `options.mobile: false` overrides detection\n- All four palm-rejection scenarios fire through without crashing\n- Mode toggle mid-stroke doesn't crash\n\nThe actual palm-rejection effectiveness needs real device testing \u2014 synthetic events in Node can't faithfully reproduce browser-specific `PointerEvent.width` reporting quirks across iOS Safari, iPadOS, Chrome on Android, etc. But the logic is straightforward enough to review by reading the `_isPalmEvent()` function: each of the three filters has a clear gate, and they short-circuit correctly.",

Above the script tags is a clearly labeled HTML comment block:\n\n```html\n<!-- ============================================================ -->\n<!-- JAVASCRIPT \u2014 separated into the library + the UI wiring.        -->\n<!-- The HTML above is markup + CSS only. To swap in a different     -->\n<!-- UI on top of the same simulation, replace just the wiring file. -->\n<!-- ============================================================ -->\n<script src=\"watercolor-lib.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"watercolor-v0.28-wiring.js\"></script>\n```\n\nThe wiring file's header documents the three-file architecture, the required load order, and the section ordering. The HTML is now markup + CSS only \u2014 clean separation.\n\n**2. Hidden console output.** Removed the `console.log('Watercolor v0.27 \u00b7 lib v2 \u00b7 \u2026')` banner that fired on every page load. The two remaining `console.warn` / `console.error` calls are in error-handling paths only (failed `scale()` validation, failed SVG parse) \u2014 those should stay since silencing them on actual errors makes debugging harder. The lib's WebGL warnings only fire when WebGL2 init actually fails, which won't happen on modern browsers.\n\n**3. Initial splotches removed.** Deleted these from the wiring:\n\n```js\nconst w0 = wc.grid.width, h0 = wc.grid.height;\nwc.paintAt(w0 * 0.40, h0 * 0.50, w0 * 0.04, 0, 0.7);\nwc.paintAt(w0 * 0.60, h0 * 0.50, w0 * 0.04, 2, 0.7);\n```\n\nCanvas now opens blank.\n\n**4. Mobile-scrollable controls.** Two CSS additions:\n\n```css\n@media (max-width: 520px) {\n  .controls {\n    max-height: calc(100dvh - 24px);   /* dvh = dynamic viewport height */\n    overflow-y: auto;\n    -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch; /* iOS momentum */\n    overscroll-behavior: contain;      /* don't scroll-chain to body */\n  }\n}\nbody.wc-mobile .controls {\n  max-height: calc(100dvh - 24px);\n  overflow-y: auto;\n  -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;\n  overscroll-behavior: contain;\n}\n```\n\nThe viewport-cap uses `dvh` (dynamic viewport height) so the panel stays sized correctly as the iOS address bar shows/hides. `overscroll-behavior: contain` prevents the panel scroll from chaining to the body (which would cause page bounce on iOS).\n\nThe `.wc-mobile` body class (set by the wiring when mobile mode is on) means desktop users with the toggle flipped on also get scrollable behavior \u2014 useful for testing the mobile UX on a desktop browser.\n\nAlso removed the auto-collapse logic I'd added in the last v0.28 round \u2014 you didn't ask for it, and scrolling solves the off-screen problem more directly without hiding controls.",

A header explanation comment block (the boxed legend), then:\n3. `BEGIN watercolor-lib.js` \u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2557 comment marker  \n   \u2192 inline `<script>` containing the lib  \n   \u2192 `END watercolor-lib.js` \u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2557 comment marker\n4. `BEGIN watercolor-v0.28-wiring.js` \u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2557 comment marker  \n   \u2192 inline `<script>` containing the UI wiring  \n   \u2192 `END watercolor-v0.28-wiring.js` \u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2557 comment marker\n\nThe two script blocks are independent \u2014 each could be `Ctrl+A`-copied out and saved as a standalone `.js` file, then loaded via `<script src>` instead. The headers say so explicitly. There are also explicit `// === BEGIN ... ===` / `// === END ... ===` JS comments inside each script that match the HTML markers, so a regex extraction script can target either layer.\n\n**Real bug I caught during the build:** the wiring file's documentation header contained the literal text `</script>` inside a JS comment showing the example loading order. When inlined, the browser's HTML parser doesn't know that text is inside a comment \u2014 it sees `</script>` and closes the script tag right there. The whole rest of the wiring (20,000+ characters) ended up rendered as page text. The fix is the standard `<\\/script>` escape \u2014 the JS parser sees the same thing (a regular comment), but the HTML parser no longer terminates early. Verified afterward: both blocks parse correctly at full size (~207KB lib, ~20KB wiring).\n\nThe previous three-file output is gone \u2014 `/mnt/user-data/outputs/watercolor-v0.28.html` is now a single 343KB self-contained file. The separate `watercolor-v0.28-wiring.js` has been removed.",

      "new_str": "function applyPendingPaint() {\n  if (_pendingPaintX === null) {\n    // No new position queued. If a stroke is still in progress AND\n    // continuous flow is on, drip at the held position.\n    if (_isPainting && _continuousFlow) {\n      const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n      paintAt(_lastGX, _lastGY, r, currentPigment, CONTINUOUS_FLOW_STRENGTH);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n  const gx = _pendingPaintX, gy = _pendingPaintY;\n  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  // v0.28 \u2014 stamp spacing = r * _brushFlow (was hardcoded 0.4). Smaller\n  // _brushFlow \u2192 more stamps per stroke \u2192 smoother continuous look;\n  // larger \u2192 fewer stamps \u2192 dabbed/dotted look.       "description": "SVG trace uses active brush settings + flipY option",
      "new_str": "// v0.28 \u2014 SVG trace now uses the active brush instead of hardcoded\n// SVG_TRACE_BRUSH_PX / SVG_TRACE_STRENGTH. Optional opts override per-\n// call: { pigment, brushSize, strength, flipY }.\n//\n// flipY default = true. Some SVG sources (CAD exports, plot-style\n// diagrams, files originally drawn in Cartesian-Y) end up looking\n// upside-down when traced because their authors used Y-up convention\n// despite SVG's official Y-down spec. Most consumer SVGs (Illustrator,\n// Inkscape, Figma, web design) are already Y-down. Flipping vertically\n// turned out to be the more common desired behavior on real-world\n// hand-drawn SVGs, so it's the default \u2014 opt out with flipY: false.\nfunction loadSVGAndTrace(svgText, opts) {\n  opts = opts || {};\n  const parser = new DOMParser();\n  const doc = parser.parseFromString(svgText, 'image/svg+xml');\n  const svg = doc.querySelector('svg');\n  if (!svg) throw new Error('No <svg> root found');\n\n  let viewBox = svg.getAttribute('viewBox');\n  let vbX = 0, vbY = 0, vbW = +(svg.getAttribute('width') || 100), vbH = +(svg.getAttribute('height') || 100);\n  if (viewBox) {\n    const parts = viewBox.trim().split(/[\\s,]+/).map(Number);\n    if (parts.length === 4) { vbX = parts[0]; vbY = parts[1]; vbW = parts[2]; vbH = parts[3]; }\n  }\n\n  const svgNS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';\n  const tmpSvg = document.createElementNS(svgNS, 'svg');\n  document.body.appendChild(tmpSvg);\n\n  const allPoints = [];\n  const geomEls = svg.querySelectorAll('path,line,polyline,polygon,rect,circle,ellipse');\n  for (const el of geomEls) {\n    const d = pathStringFromSvgElement(el);\n    if (!d) continue;\n    const p = document.createElementNS(svgNS, 'path');\n    p.setAttribute('d', d);\n    tmpSvg.appendChild(p);\n    let len = 0;\n    try { len = p.getTotalLength(); } catch (e) { tmpSvg.removeChild(p); continue; }\n    if (!isFinite(len) || len <= 0) { tmpSvg.removeChild(p); continue; }\n    const samples = Math.max(2, Math.ceil(len / 1));\n    for (let s = 0; s <= samples; s++) {\n      const pt = p.getPointAtLength((s / samples) * len);\n      allPoints.push({ x: pt.x, y: pt.y });\n    }\n    tmpSvg.removeChild(p);\n  }\n  document.body.removeChild(tmpSvg);\n  if (allPoints.length === 0) return 0;\n\n  // Fit into SVG_TRACE_FIT of the canvas.\n  const sx = (GW * SVG_TRACE_FIT) / vbW;\n  const sy = (GH * SVG_TRACE_FIT) / vbH;\n  const s = Math.min(sx, sy);\n  const offsetX = GW * 0.5 - (vbW * s) * 0.5;\n  const offsetY = GH * 0.5 - (vbH * s) * 0.5;\n\n  // v0.28 \u2014 flipY: when true, mirror Y around the SVG's vertical\n  // center, fixing upside-down traces from Y-up SVG sources. Default\n  // true. The math: standard Y is `(pt.y - vbY) * s`; flipped is\n  // `(vbH - (pt.y - vbY)) * s`.\n  const flipY = opts.flipY !== undefined ? !!opts.flipY : true;\n  const scaledPoints = allPoints.map(pt => ({\n    x: offsetX + (pt.x - vbX) * s,\n    y: offsetY + (flipY ? (vbH - (pt.y - vbY)) : (pt.y - vbY)) * s,\n  }));\n\n  // Down-sample to roughly one point per SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS grid cells.\n  const downSampled = [];\n  let lastX = -1e9, lastY = -1e9;\n  for (const pt of scaledPoints) {\n    const dx = pt.x - lastX, dy = pt.y - lastY;\n    if (dx * dx + dy * dy >= SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS * SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS) {\n      downSampled.push(pt);\n      lastX = pt.x; lastY = pt.y;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // v0.28 \u2014 Use the active brush instead of hardcoded constants.\n  // Resolve pigment by name if a string was supplied (e.g. 'rose'),\n  // otherwise default to the currently-active pigment. Same for size,\n  // strength \u2014 fall through to live state if not specified.\n  const pigment = opts.pigment !== undefined\n    ? _resolvePigmentOption(opts.pigment)\n    : currentPigment;\n  const brushPx = opts.brushSize !== undefined ? +opts.brushSize : brushSize;\n  const strength = opts.strength !== undefined ? +opts.strength : _brushPressure;\n  // Brush radius in grid units. Match the same formula pointer-down\n  // uses: (diameter / 2) / SCALE.\n  const brushR = (brushPx / 2) / SCALE;\n\n  svgTraceState = {\n    points: downSampled,\n    idx: 0,\n    pigment: pigment,\n    brushR: brushR,\n    strength: strength,\n  };\n  markCanvasActive();\n  return downSampled.length;\n}",

Three knobs that previously lived as\n  // hardcoded constants in the pointer-event handlers and SVG tracer.\n  //\n  // pressure(v) \u2014 base stamp strength, 0..1. Default 0.7. Lower\n  //   values dim every stamp (lighter brush); higher saturate faster.\n  //   Applied uniformly across the stroke (set at pointer-down).\n  pressure(v) {\n    if (v === undefined) return _brushPressure;\n    _brushPressure = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, +v));\n    return _brushPressure;\n  },\n  // flow(v) \u2014 stamp spacing along a drag, as a fraction of brush\n  //   radius. Default 0.4. Smaller = more stamps = smoother strokes\n  //   (and more sim cost). Larger = fewer stamps = dotted/dabbed look.\n  //   Range clamped to 0.05..2.0 to prevent pathological behavior.\n  flow(v) {\n    if (v === undefined) return _brushFlow;\n    _brushFlow = Math.max(0.05, Math.min(2.0, +v));\n    return _brushFlow;\n  },\n  // usePointerPressure(v) \u2014 when true, multiplies stamp strength by\n  //   PointerEvent.pressure. Useful for stylus + tablet users; mostly\n  //   harmless for mouse since the resulting strength is captured at\n  //   pointer-down and held for the rest of the stroke. Default false\n  //   because mouse browsers commonly report 0.5 (neutral) \u2014 turning\n  //   this on without a stylus would shift the base by *1.0 (no change)\n  //   but the variability per-event would create unintended dimming.\n  usePointerPressure(v) {\n    if (v === undefined) return _usePointerPressure;\n    _usePointerPressure = !!v;\n    return _usePointerPressure;\n  },\n  // continuousFlow(v) \u2014 when on, holding the pointer in place keeps\n  //   pumping pigment/water at a reduced rate (CONTINUOUS_FLOW_STRENGTH\n  //   = 0.12). Mimics how a real brush bleeds when held still on wet\n  //   paper.
